segments.beat: Hand holds device face-on toward camera; lit display showing pixel game content visible through transparent pane; clear shell reveals PCB/internals underneath
Device rotated and tilted; golden PCB board fully visible through clear resin shell from side/rear angle; display still partially lit
Continued rotation showing underside and rear of device; transparent shell from multiple angles exposes full board layout; D-pad and A/B buttons visible on face
Camera pulls slightly back; device held in palm showing pocket scale; shell internals and display face both in frame
Close macro shot of display face; see-through display lit with pixel/icon content (snowflake-style graphics); transparent pane clearly shows game pixels floating on clear background
Device angled to show profile; golden board and component traces visible through clear shell at oblique angle; display active
Return to overhead/table angle; device placed in hand against neutral background; full form factor visible, controls and transparent body clear

demonstrates.motion_value: Rotation through multiple angles lets viewer track the golden PCB and component traces through the shell continuously — a still only shows one angle; motion proves the transparency is full 360 not a windowed cutout

Macro approach and slight angle shift reveals pixel content (snowflake/icon graphics) visibly floating on a transparent pane with background visible behind them — motion confirms it is genuinely see-through, not a reflective effect
Display shows active pixel game content from the first frame; D-pad and A/B layout glimpsed as hand repositions — motion confirms the controls are physical and the screen is live
Brief pull-back shows device sitting in a single palm; scale is established relative to hand size — motion transitions from close to context shot making the size read clearly
Slow deliberate rotation mimics the way someone turns over an object they find beautiful — the handling gesture itself signals it is an object worth inspecting, not just using
As shell catches light during rotation, resin clarity and edge sharpness are visible; motion catches specular highlights that confirm optical-grade finish

selection_note: 13s hand-rotation demo nails both load-bearing claims (internals through shell + lit transparent display) in a single continuous motion; trims cleanly to a 6-8s hero loop on the macro display beat.

aesthetics.standout: display legible in close-up frames around 0:06-0:08
aesthetics.weakness: handheld prototype demo on bare desk - no controlled framing or cinematic intent
aesthetics.note: raw maker footage; hand holding device over a wood-grain desk with flat overhead light - needs a clean-surface tripod reshoot with intentional lighting before paid placement
